title: Volume phase holographic waveguide for display
abstract: A holographic waveguide display may be capable of projecting high definition, full color images, generated using broad bandwidth wavelength light sources, through an optically transmissive or "see-through" media to a viewer's eye. The holographic waveguide employs volume phase holographic (VPH) diffraction gratings in reflection. The holographic waveguide enables delivery of wavelength and angle of incidence (AOI) selective information through a transparent material, allowing the user an unobstructed view of the real world environment with the addition of rich media content superimposed on the field of regard, hence facilitating the user experience often termed "augmented reality". The holographic waveguide may include a Y- waveguide that increases the vertical dimension of the eye box by shifting or offsetting extreme field angles spatially.
claims:
Claims 1. A holographic waveguide for use in a display comprising: a transparent substrate; a thin film photopolymer; a transparent cover layer; an adhesive layer attaching the cover layer to the photopolymer and substrate; an in-coupling volume phase hologram recorded in the photopolymer at a first location multiplexing three optical functions into the photopolymer; each function responsible for steering an associated spectral bandwidth range of visible wavelengths down a length of the waveguide at angles that achieve total internal reflection; a waveguide stack of materials with matched index of refraction, capable of accepting light of multiple visible wavelengths from the in-coupling hologram and transmitting the light along a length of the substrate through total internal reflection; and an out-coupling volume phase hologram recorded in the photopolymer at a second location multiplexing three optical functions into the photopolymer, each function responsible for steering an associated spectral bandwidth range of visible wavelengths out of the waveguide toward a viewer's eye and correcting for chromatic dispersion induced in the in-coupling volume phase hologram. 2. The waveguide of claim 1 , wherein said in-coupling hologram and out-coupling hologram are recorded to enable a field-of-view of at least 45Â° at an eye relief distance of 25mm. 3. The waveguide of claim 1 , wherein said in-coupling hologram and out-coupling hologram are recorded to cause total internal reflection in the waveguide producing multiple exit pupil's and a large composite exit pupil with a horizontal 

 eye box at least 15mm in size and a vertical eye box at least 10mm at an eye relief distance of 25mm. 4. The waveguide of claim 1 , wherein said in-coupling hologram and out-coupling hologram are recorded and processed to be at least 90% transparent in the visible spectrum. 5. The waveguide of claim 1 , wherein the virtual image presented to the viewer is located beyond the hyperfocal distance of the human eye. 6. The waveguide of claim 1 , wherein the virtual image presented to the viewer is located at a predetermined virtual image distance. 7. The waveguide of claim 1 , wherein the light directed to the viewer is composed of a complex wavefront. 8. The waveguide of claim 1 , wherein the substrate, photopolymer, and cover are curved along a single axis. 9. The waveguide of claim 1, wherein the in-coupling and out-coupling holograms are color multiplexed to include more than three optical functions corresponding to specific projection wavelength bands and field of view. 10. The waveguide of claim 1, wherein two or more holograms are stacked to diffract different zones of a larger field of view. 11. The waveguide of claim 1, wherein the in-coupling hologram is located near the edge on waveguide, offset horizontally from the viewer's normal line of sight. 12. The waveguide of claim 1, wherein the waveguide is compatible with prescription lenses. 13. The waveguide of claim 1, wherein the photopolymer layer is replaced by dichromated gelatin or other photorefractive material. 14. The waveguide of claim 1, wherein the in-coupling and out-coupling volume phase holograms comprise transmission volume phase holograms. 15. The waveguide of claim 1 , wherein the in-coupling hologram comprises an edge- lit hologram or a refractive prism. 16. The waveguide of claim 1 , wherein the substrate and cover layer comprise a material selected from the group consisting of: glass and plastic. 17. The waveguide of claim 1, further comprising a vertical direction hologram located between the in-coupling hologram and the out- coupling hologram. 18. A holographic waveguide for use in a display comprising: a transparent substrate; a thin film photopolymer; a transparent cover layer; an adhesive layer attaching the cover layer to the photopolymer and substrate; a waveguide stack of materials with matched index of refraction, capable of accepting multiple visible wavelengths; a first volume phase hologram recorded in the photopolymer multiplexing three optical functions into the single layer; each function responsible for steering an associated spectral bandwidth range of visible wavelengths down a length of the 

 waveguide at angles that achieve total internal reflection; a second volume phase hologram that diffracts light at extreme field angles of the vertical field of view and directs the light toward a mid-plane of the waveguide; and a third volume phase hologram that diffracts light at total internal reflection field angles and directs the light out of the waveguide at the input angle. 19. A method for increasing the field of view presented to the viewer beyond that listed in claim 2, by employing selective spectral illumination across the field of view. 20. A method for achieving color uniformity and white balance across a display field of view by controlling red, green, and blue brightness at an individual pixel level.
